one_line_description: CPaaS platform for programmable SMS, voice, WhatsApp, email, and verification via APIs.
auth_summary: REST APIs authenticate with HTTP Basic auth using Account SID + Auth Token, or (preferred for production) API keys (Main/Standard/Restricted) used as Basic auth credentials.
access_notes: Self-serve signup with a free trial account; trial has restrictions (verified numbers, trial message watermark). Full sending requires funding the account and, for some channels, regulatory/sender registration (e.g. A2P 10DLC).
api_surface_summary: REST APIs over HTTPS covering messaging, voice calls, phone number lookup/provisioning, verification, and more, with official SDKs in many languages.
agent_notes: Official docs confirm API keys are the preferred auth. Twilio Labs publishes an official (alpha) MCP server exposing 1,400+ endpoints generated from OpenAPI specs. Carrier/regulatory registration affects real-world SMS sending but not API buildability.
